title: Pass 5.A.4 follow-up: wire gpu_collective NCCL fast path (GpuTensor → raw pointer → NcclBackend)
description:
Replaces closed #668 (Fix 11 silent GPU->CPU fallbacks gated on PtxCompileFailed + rewrite REDUCE_SUM_PTX/REDUCE_PROD_PTX shared-memory addressing).

Current state: `gpu_allreduce` and `gpu_broadcast` in `ferrotorch-distributed/src/gpu_collective.rs` have `#[cfg(feature = "nccl")]` placeholder arms that compile to no-ops. Without the `nccl` feature and without `FERROTORCH_ENABLE_GPU_FALLBACK=1`, the functions return `Err(DistributedError::UnsupportedOp)` (PyTorch parity).

Real implementation requires cross-crate glue to:
1. Extract the raw device pointer from `GpuTensor<T>` (lives in `ferrotorch-gpu`)
2. Dispatch through `NcclBackend::allreduce_raw` / `broadcast_raw` (lives in `ferrotorch-distributed::nccl_backend`)

The 6 tests at gpu_collective.rs:243/293/336/379/392/403 are currently `#[ignore]` because they relied on the silent fallback that ADR #663 item 7 made opt-in. They become runnable once either (a) the NCCL wiring lands here, OR (b) a test harness sets `FERROTORCH_ENABLE_GPU_FALLBACK` for the affected tests.
